archetype: Real-Time In-Vivo Chiral Purity & Enantiomeric Excess (ee) Monitor for Continuous Flow Biocatalysis
description: A deterministic edge-computing system that ingests terabytes of high-frequency spectroscopic data from inline chiral sensor arrays to calculate enantiomeric purity in millisecond intervals, enabling immediate feedback control of micro-reactor flow rates and enzyme concentrations. It is technically demanding because it requires sub-millisecond latency to prevent the production of costly off-spec enantiomers in a continuous stream where reaction time is measured in seconds, not hours.
rules: MUST implement IEC 61508 SIL-3 compliant fail-safe logic to trigger immediate reactor flush if chirality drifts beyond 0.5% threshold.
MUST process data streams conforming to the Analytical Method Exchange (AMX) standard and OPC UA PubSub for hardware abstraction.
MUST enforce FDA 21 CFR Part 11 electronic signature and audit trail immutability for every control parameter adjustment.
MUST utilize hard real-time operating system (RTOS) primitives to guarantee deterministic jitter under 100 microseconds.
MUST integrate with industrial PID loops via Modbus TCP and EtherCAT protocols for direct actuator control without cloud round-trips.
anti_patterns: DO NOT use asynchronous event loops or garbage-collected languages with stop-the-world pauses for the control loop, as latency spikes can ruin an entire batch.
DO NOT rely on cloud-based inference for critical control decisions, as network jitter violates the strict timing constraints of continuous flow chemistry.
DO NOT implement standard SQL transactional locking for high-frequency sensor data writes, as the overhead prevents the system from keeping up with 100kHz sampling rates.
key_technical_challenges: Performing real-time Fourier Transform Infrared (FTIR) signal deconvolution on embedded hardware to distinguish overlapping chiral peaks.
Stabilizing control loops against stochastic noise from flow pumps while maintaining sub-second response times to exothermic reaction spikes.
Ensuring data integrity and cryptographic signing of high-volume sensor telemetry streams while strictly adhering to GMP audit requirements.

recommended_stack.language: Rust — Provides memory safety guarantees without a garbage collector, essential for hard real-time deterministic execution.
recommended_stack.framework: Rust-RTOS (e.g., Tock or Zephyr) — Offers microsecond-level scheduling precision and hardware abstraction.
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB with hypertables partitioned by reaction batch ID — Optimized for high-velocity time-series ingestion with SQL querying.
recommended_stack.cache: None — Data must be processed in-flight; caching introduces unacceptable latency for control decisions.
recommended_stack.message_broker: Eclipse Paho MQTT over TLS 1.3 — Low overhead, reliable delivery for telemetry to remote monitoring stations.
special_services: Embedded DSP Kernel for Spectral deconvolution
